description: In Turkish, velar stop consonants /k/ and /g/ have palatal allophones [c] and [ɟ] before front vowels (/e, i, ø, y/). This allophonic variation is part of Turkish vowel harmony, where the place of articulation shifts to match the front quality of the following vowel.
sources: Zimmer, K., & Orgun, O. (1999). _Turkish_. In _Handbook of the International Phonetic Association: A Guide to the Use of the International Phonetic Alphabet_. Cambridge University Press.
Lewis, G. L. (2001). _Turkish Grammar_. Oxford University Press.
